title: Intensely Spicy Indian Lentil Paratha
description: A spicy and flaky Hyderabadi dish featuring red lentils and a hint of cream, perfect for the monsoon season, this recipe is vegan-friendly and cooked using a bain-marie method to achieve the flakiest parathas.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour, melted ghee or vegan butter, and salt, and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, combine the cooked red lentils, chopped green chili peppers, grated ginger, and minced garlic, and mix well.
Add the cream or non-dairy alternative to the lentil mixture and stir to combine.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of the lentil mixture in the center of each dough circle and fold the dough over to form a triangle or square shape.
Brush the tops of the parathas with a little water and cook them in a bain-marie over boiling water for 5-7 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and flaky.
Serve the parathas hot with a dollop of cream or non-dairy alternative and a sprinkle of cumin seeds.
